varieties.name: Black Gold
varieties.traits: Medium-sized fruit averaging 6-7kg. Dark green skin with sharp fleshy spines that do not flatten at maturity. Vigorous tree with dense, manageable canopy.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep orange flesh, soft texture. Sweet flavor with pleasant aroma. 35% edible flesh percentage.
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producer, 55-90kg per tree annually with pruning
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent subtropical performance. Can be maintained at 2-2.5m height. Tolerates Brisbane's occasional cool weather. Popular in Queensland nurseries. Used as rootstock.
varieties.availability: Daleys Fruit Tree Nursery, Queensland nurseries, widely available Brisbane region

varieties.name: Golden Nugget
varieties.traits: Round, small to medium fruit, yellow skin, averaging 3kg. Fast-growing tree with distinctive dark green, rounded leaves.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Dark orange flesh with medium firmness. Excellent flavor, butter-soft when fully ripe. No fiber.
varieties.yield_notes: 60-80kg per tree per year, consistent producer
varieties.growing_notes: Adapts well to subtropical Brisbane climate. May split during heavy rains. Good humidity tolerance. Popular with Australian growers.
varieties.availability: Queensland fruit tree nurseries, Brisbane suppliers

varieties.name: Honey Gold
varieties.traits: Small to medium fruit, 2-3kg. Golden-yellow skin. Dwarf cultivar, compact tree.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden flesh with honey-like sweetness. Peachy interior, soft texture. Exceptionally sweet.
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane's subtropical conditions. Small size ideal for home gardens. Heat and humidity tolerant. Dwarf growth habit reduces space requirements.
varieties.availability: Specialty fruit nurseries Queensland, Brisbane area

varieties.name: Lemon Gold
varieties.traits: Unique block/square shape, 12-13kg fruit. Yellow skin at maturity.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm, crisp texture. Extraordinarily sweet flavor.

varieties.name: Brinsmead Special
varieties.origin: Kyogle, Northern NSW/QLD border region. Selected by Bob Brinsmead at Tropical Fruit World
varieties.traits: Latex-free variety (major advantage). Crisp orange flesh. Chance seedling selection.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Orange crisp flesh, easy to prepare due to latex-free properties.
varieties.growing_notes: Outstanding subtropical adaptation. Grows well in Brisbane climate zone. Latex-free makes processing much easier. Best selection from Tropical Fruit World.

varieties.name: Cosmic Gold
varieties.traits: Sweet variety with grafted reliability.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow/gold flesh with vanilla, banana and mango flavors.
varieties.growing_notes: Brisbane-suitable. Sweet complex flavor profile. Available as grafted trees which fruit earlier.
varieties.availability: Edibles Only Nursery Inala Brisbane, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Sunshine Crunch / Ratu Red
varieties.traits: Very large fruit variety. Crunchy texture type.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Gold to pink/red flesh depending on ripeness. Crunchy texture.
varieties.growing_notes: Adapted to Queensland conditions. Novel flesh color. Good subtropical performance.

varieties.name: Galaxy
varieties.traits: Crunchy variety, grafted for consistency.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Superior flavor, crunchy texture. Good for both curries and fresh eating.
varieties.yield_notes: Productive, fruits in 1-2 years from grafted
varieties.growing_notes: Sun-hardened for Queensland conditions. Handles subtropical weather well. Versatile use.

varieties.name: Rajang
varieties.traits: Orange crisp flesh type. Larger fruit, later maturing than Amber.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Orange, crisp texture. Good quality.
varieties.growing_notes: Bred for Australian tropical/subtropical conditions. Performs well in Queensland. Heat and humidity tolerant. Commands higher market prices.

varieties.name: Amber
varieties.traits: Orange crisp flesh type. Earlier maturing than Rajang.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Orange, crisp texture.
varieties.growing_notes: Australian-bred for subtropical conditions. Earlier fruiting advantage. Well-adapted to Brisbane region.

varieties.name: Fitzroy Selection
varieties.traits: Large fruit. Selected for quality.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow crisp flesh with excellent flavor. 99% excellent rating at Rockhampton Horticultural display.
varieties.growing_notes: Selected specifically for Central Queensland conditions. Highly rated for flavor. Subtropical adapted.

varieties.name: Kyogle Selection (Soft Type)
varieties.traits: Choice seedling selection. Soft aril type.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft arils perfect for drying or freezing. Superior flavor. Rich texture without stringy fibers.
varieties.growing_notes: Proven subtropical performance. Selected from area with similar climate to Brisbane. Easy to preserve.

varieties.name: Daleys Favourite
varieties.traits: Small sized fruit, about 2kg, packed with arils.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft flesh, rich and delicious. Hints of banana, pineapple and tropical flavors. No stringy fibers.
varieties.growing_notes: Selected as nursery favorite for Queensland. Manageable fruit size. Excellent eating quality. Subtropical performer.

varieties.name: Meadowcroft Farm Selection
varieties.traits: Selected from multiple trees grown in same conditions.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Orange crunchy flesh in abundance. Little latex. Tropical flavors like pineapple and mango. Even rags are edible.
varieties.growing_notes: Field tested in Queensland conditions. Low latex advantage. Described as addictive. December fruiting season. Supplies southern markets.

varieties.name: Northern NSW Vigorous Selection
varieties.traits: Vigorous selection with dense, highly manageable canopy.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep orange aril flesh, soft. Strong, sweet flavor and aroma.
varieties.growing_notes: Frost-free subtropical locations. Can be pruned compact. Consistent cropper. Similar climate to Brisbane.

varieties.name: Vietnam Super Early
varieties.traits: Remarkably fast-bearing, produces fruit in just over 1 year. Dwarf variety, doesn't grow too large.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality fruit.
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane's subtropical climate. Small size makes fruit picking easy. Cold-hardy for a tropical fruit. Manageable tree size for home gardens.

varieties.name: Cochin
varieties.origin: Australia (selected from Indian Kerala region genetics)
varieties.traits: Low vigor tree, sparse upright narrow canopy. Heritage variety characteristics.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft and juicy bulbs. Aromatic ripe fruit.

varieties.name: Dang Rasimi
varieties.traits: Bright green to pale yellow fruit, 8kg average. Sharp spines that don't flatten. Uniform shape when thinned. Very vigorous, requires annual pruning.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep orange, firm flesh. Mild sweet flavor, pleasant aroma. 32% edible percentage.
varieties.growing_notes: Can be maintained at 3-3.5m with pruning. Excellent subtropical adaptation. Popular internationally. Good humidity tolerance.

varieties.name: J-31
varieties.traits: Large irregular fruit with prominent blunt spines, 12kg. Moderately vigorous, spreading canopy.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep yellow, firm flesh with thick walls. Sweet rich flavor, strong earthy aroma. 36% edible.
varieties.yield_notes: 42-60kg per year at 2-2.5m size. Off-season fruiting capability.
varieties.growing_notes: Cold resistance advantage. Sweet rich flavor. Adapts to subtropical Brisbane conditions. Can produce fall/winter fruit.
varieties.availability: Imported variety available through specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Cheena
varieties.traits: Long narrow uniform fruit, 2.4kg. Green skin with blunt spines that yellow at maturity. Open low spreading habit.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep orange, soft somewhat fibrous. Excellent flavor. Intense earthy aroma. 33% edible. Quick preparation (15 minutes vs 1-2 hours).
varieties.growing_notes: Can be maintained at 2.5m. Easy processing major advantage. Subtropical adapted. Unique hybrid characteristics.

varieties.name: Golden Pillow (Mong Tong)
varieties.traits: Small highly manageable tree, 3m height and spread. Medium fruit 3.6-5.5kg. Little latex.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Thick crunchy golden flesh. Mild sweet flavor, no musky aftertaste. 35-40% edible. 65-75 seeds.
varieties.yield_notes: Precocious fruiting - bears in second year
varieties.growing_notes: Ideal for Brisbane gardens due to small size. Quick bearing. Low latex advantage. Reputation for beauty and quality in Thailand.

varieties.name: Ceylon / Singapore Jack 🌱
varieties.traits: Remarkably early bearer - produces in 18 months to 2.5 years from transplanting. Medium size fruit.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Small fibrous carpels, very sweet.

varieties.name: Muttom Varikka 🏆
varieties.origin: Muttom locality, Alappuzha district, Kerala (Kerala Agricultural University release)

varieties.name: Varikka (General Type) 🏆
varieties.traits: One of two main indigenous Kerala types. Premium category.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm bulbs, crisp crunchy texture. More flesh than koozha type. Intense flavor. Color ranges cream to deep saffron/orange. Better preserved.

varieties.name: Koozha (General Type) 🏆
varieties.traits: One of two main indigenous Kerala types.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft bulbs, fibrous, spongy to mushy texture. More moisture than varikka. Less fleshy. Softer, moister, much sweeter. Darker gold-color flesh.
varieties.growing_notes: Traditional Kerala classification. Contrast to varikka. Some prefer for extreme sweetness and soft texture. Both types processed for tender jackfruit canning. Monsoon-adapted. Suitable for Thiruvalla. Higher antioxidants in raw bulbs.

varieties.name: Chirappuram Selections 🏆
varieties.traits: Local selections from Kerala. Multiple clones identified.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm texture, very sweet flavor. Excellent quality.

varieties.name: Panruti 🏆
varieties.origin: Panruti region, Cuddalore district, Tamil Nadu (jackfruit capital of India)
varieties.traits: Famous variety from India's largest jackfruit region. Cultivated year-round as mono-crop. 800+ hectares.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet honey-like flavor. Firm texture. Excellent quality.

varieties.name: Tenkasi 🏆
varieties.traits: Well-known Tamil Nadu variety. Appreciated by jackfruit enthusiasts.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm bulbs with intense sweetness.
varieties.growing_notes: Preferred choice for quality. Tamil Nadu heritage. Similar tropical climate to Kerala. Suitable for monsoon regions including Thiruvalla.

varieties.name: Gumless Variety 🌱
varieties.traits: Famous for minimal latex. State and national branding potential.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Minimal sticky sap/latex - major processing advantage.
varieties.growing_notes: Revolutionary characteristic - no gum/latex makes processing easy. Excellent for Kerala conditions. Suitable for Thiruvalla. Commercial potential. All characters required for major branding.

varieties.name: Sadananda Halasu 🌱
varieties.traits: All-season variety - fruits multiple times per year.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good quality flesh.
varieties.growing_notes: Unique all-season fruiting characteristic. Monsoon-adapted. Suitable for Kerala climate including Thiruvalla. Continuous production potential.

varieties.name: Maharajapuram 🏆
varieties.traits: Traditional variety. Regional importance.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Quality flesh characteristics.

varieties.name: Rudrakshi / Rudrakshi Red 🌱
varieties.traits: Named variety from Karnataka. Red-hued type.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Small sweet bulbs. Tender texture. Red coloring.
varieties.growing_notes: Mainly consumed fresh. Karnataka heritage but adaptable to Kerala monsoon conditions. Suitable for Thiruvalla.
varieties.availability: Karnataka nurseries, may be in Kerala

varieties.name: Mankale Red 🌱
varieties.traits: Named after Mankale region. Red-hued variety.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Beautiful red flesh coloring.

varieties.name: Mijar Giant 🌱
varieties.traits: Large fruit variety. Giant type.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Large fruit with substantial flesh.
varieties.growing_notes: Karnataka heritage. Tropical adapted. Can thrive in Kerala monsoon conditions including Thiruvalla.

varieties.name: Varashree Jack 🌱
varieties.traits: Named Karnataka variety.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Quality flesh characteristics.
varieties.growing_notes: Karnataka cultivar. Tropical monsoon adapted. Suitable for Kerala conditions.

varieties.name: Byrachandra / Byrachandra Super 🌱
varieties.traits: Superior selection. Quality variety.
varieties.flesh_fruit: High quality flesh.
varieties.growing_notes: Found in both Karnataka and Malaysia. Tropical climate adapted. Suitable for Kerala monsoon conditions including Thiruvalla.

varieties.name: Hemachandra 🌱
varieties.traits: Common cultivar in jackfruit village. Large fruits possible (up to 100 pounds in region).
varieties.flesh_fruit: Quality flesh.
varieties.growing_notes: Tamil Nadu heritage. Tropical monsoon adapted. Suitable for Kerala including Thiruvalla.

varieties.name: Janagere Yellow 🌱
varieties.traits: Yellow flesh type.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Yellow colored flesh.
varieties.growing_notes: Found in multiple regions. Tropical adapted. Suitable for Kerala monsoon climate.

varieties.name: Chandra
varieties.traits: Developed for consistent size and quality. Hybrid variety.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Consistent quality flesh.

varieties.name: Barka 🏆
varieties.traits: Popular eastern India variety. Among 35 superior clones identified from West Bengal.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet firm bulbs. Excellent for fresh consumption.

varieties.name: Waraka (Sri Lankan) 🏆
varieties.traits: Firm variety classification. Crunchy with crisp flakes. Considered more desirable and high quality.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm flesh remains firm even at full ripeness. Crunchy crisp texture. Color ranges cream to deep saffron/orange. Better preserved.
varieties.yield_notes: Good production, 10-200 fruits per mature tree
varieties.growing_notes: Traditional Sri Lankan type equivalent to Indian varikka. Excellent monsoon adaptation. Similar climate requirements to Kerala. Suitable for Thiruvalla. Better shelf life than soft types.
varieties.availability: Sri Lankan nurseries, may be available in Kerala

varieties.name: Wela (Sri Lankan) 🏆
varieties.origin: Sri Lanka (called Koozha in India). Predominant in West Indies.
varieties.traits: Soft variety classification.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft fleshy perianths on ripening. Soft, rubbery, spongy texture. Very sweet. Small fibrous carpels. Darker gold color than firm varieties.
varieties.growing_notes: Traditional Sri Lankan type equivalent to Indian koozha. Very sweet preferred by some. Monsoon-adapted. Suitable for Kerala including Thiruvalla. Traditional genetics.

varieties.name: Peniwaraka (Honey Jackfruit) 🏆
varieties.traits: Honey jackfruit of Sri Lanka.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet pulp claimed as best of all jackfruits. Honey-sweet qualities.

varieties.name: Kuruwaraka 🏆
varieties.traits: Small rounded fruits. Dark-green rind.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden yellow pulp - contrast to dark green exterior.
varieties.growing_notes: Sri Lankan cultivar. Distinctive appearance. Monsoon-adapted. Suitable for Kerala conditions including Thiruvalla.

varieties.name: Singapore / Fatherlong 🏆
varieties.traits: Medium size fruit. Third year fruiting.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Small fibrous carpels, very sweet.

varieties.name: Maharagama 🏆
varieties.traits: Named Sri Lankan cultivar.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Quality flesh characteristics.
varieties.growing_notes: Sri Lankan heritage. Monsoon-adapted genetics. Suitable for Kerala climate.

varieties.name: Kothmale 🏆
varieties.traits: Sri Lankan cultivar.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Traditional qualities.

varieties.name: Hirosh 🏆
varieties.traits: Sri Lankan cultivar.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Quality characteristics.

varieties.name: Mandoor 🏆
varieties.traits: Sri Lankan cultivar.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Traditional qualities.
varieties.growing_notes: Sri Lankan heritage. Monsoon-adapted genetics. Suitable for Kerala conditions.

varieties.name: Thailand Pink
varieties.traits: Primary cultivar in some Kerala regions. Vibrant coloring.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink flesh - rare and vibrant. Attractive appearance.
varieties.growing_notes: Rare pink flesh jackfruit. Grows well in Kerala climate. Adapted to tropical monsoon conditions. Suitable for Thiruvalla. High market value due to novelty.

varieties.name: Dang Suriya
varieties.traits: Super tasty Thai variety. Grafted reliability.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Excellent flavor profile.

varieties.name: PP I 🌱
varieties.traits: Commonly grown in multiple Indian states.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Improved characteristics.

varieties.name: Burliar I 🌱
varieties.traits: From experimental station with 54 clone collection. Commonly grown variety.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Selected for quality.
varieties.growing_notes: Research station selection. Grown in Kerala and other states. Monsoon-adapted. Suitable for Thiruvalla.

varieties.name: Konker Prolific 🌱
varieties.traits: Prolific bearer - name indicates high productivity.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Quality fruit.
varieties.growing_notes: Commonly grown in multiple states. Known for prolific bearing. Monsoon-adapted. Suitable for Kerala including Thiruvalla.
